description_long: This is a single-story bungalow with a broad, low-pitched hip roof covered in gray shingles. The front features a spacious, tiled porch area supported by columns and enclosed by a low wall, providing a sheltered entryway. The main entrance is set back beneath the porch roof, and several rectangular windows are arranged along the visible side wall, allowing natural light into the interior. The structure is primarily composed of solid, light-colored walls with a smooth finish, and the porch area is distinctly marked by its reddish-brown tiled flooring. The rear and side elevations are more austere, with minimal window openings and a continuous expanse of wall. The overall design is compact and functional, typical of suburban or urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a hip roof, spacious front porch, and multiple rectangular windows along the side. The exterior features smooth light walls and a tiled porch area.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large covered porch, main entrance, and several side windows.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the hip roof and plain wall, with minimal window openings visible.
description_view_2: Back corner view highlighting the expanse of the roof and the solid rear and side walls.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the porch area, supporting columns, and the transition from porch to main building.
